All States · Utah

Utah Dietitian License

Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL). $60 application. 2 to 4 Weeks processing.

Last verified 2026-02-25 · Board-sourced data
↗ Official Board Portal (opens in a new tab)

Quick Facts at a Glance

Application Fee
$60
$60 nonrefundable initial application fee
Processing
2 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
Renewal
$37
Every 2 years (expires Sept 30 of even-numbered years)
License Duration
Up to 2 Years (depending on issue date relative to Sept 30 even-year cycle)
From date of issuance
Licensing Authority

Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL)

Department of Commerce
Phone
(801) 530-6628
Email
doplbureau3@utah.gov
Mailing Address
PO Box 146741
Salt Lake City, UT 84114
Physical Address
160 East 300 South, 1st Floor
Salt Lake City, UT 84111
Credential Categories

Utah Dietitian License Types

Certified Dietitian (CD)

Utah's state-recognized credential to practice dietetics. It is commonly referred to as certification rather than licensure in Utah state law.

Application Process

How to Get Licensed in Utah

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an accredited institution. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an ACEND-accredited program.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Utah requires you to hold active CDR registration.

Submit the Application via DOPL

Apply online through the Utah DOPL eGov portal or submit a paper application. Pay the $60 nonrefundable application fee.

Provide Verification

Submit a copy of your current active CDR Registration Card along with your application. Utah relies heavily on CDR registration to verify you have met the education and exam requirements.

Required Documents

Application Checklist

  • ✓
    CDR Registration Card Provide a copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration wallet card.
  • ✓
    Official Application Completed application form with the $60 fee.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules
Utah does not offer a temporary or provisional certification for dietitians. You must possess the CDR Registered Dietitian credential before applying.
Reciprocity & Endorsement
Utah offers licensure by endorsement. Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration may apply by completing the standard application and submitting a copy of their active CDR card, which proves they meet the national standards required by Utah.
Travel Dietitian Timeline
If your assignment start date is within 5 weeks, apply immediately and call the board at (801) 530-6628 to confirm current processing times.

Note: Utah does not offer a standard provisional license, so your license must be issued before your first day.
Maintenance

Renewal Requirements

Utah dietitian licenses renew every 2 years (expires sept 30 of even-numbered years). Renewal fee is $37.

Frequently Asked Questions

Utah Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Utah dietitian certification cost?

The initial cost is a $60 application fee.

When does the Utah certification need to be renewed?

Utah Certified Dietitian credentials expire on September 30 of all even-numbered years. The renewal fee is $37.

What are the continuing education requirements for Utah?

Utah accepts the continuing education completed for the maintenance of your national CDR registration (75 hours every five years) to satisfy state renewal requirements.
